description.p: Compares Version1 to Version2. The return value is less than 0 if Version1 is less than Version2, 0 if Version1 equals Version2, or greater than 0 if Version1 is greater than Version2.

function.name: SamePackedVersion
function.prototype: function SamePackedVersion(const Version1, Version2: Int64): Boolean;

description.p: Compares the packed versions Version1 and Version2 and returns True if they are equal.

function.name: UnpackVersionNumbers
function.prototype: procedure UnpackVersionNumbers(const Version: Int64; var VersionMS, VersionLS: Cardinal);

description.p: Unpacks a packed version into version numbers.

function.name: UnpackVersionComponents
function.prototype: procedure UnpackVersionComponents(const Version: Int64; var Major, Minor, Revision, Build: Word);

description.p: Unpacks a packed version into individual version components.
Returns the specified packed version as a string (in 0.0.0.0 format).

function.name: StrToVersion
function.prototype: function StrToVersion(const VersionString: String; var Version: Int64): Boolean;

description.p: Returns the specified string (in 0.0.0.0 format) as a packed version. Returns True if successful, False otherwise.
Partial version numbers (for example, '1.5') are allowed. Missing numbers will be appended as zeros. Extra numbers are ignored.

function.name: RegKeyExists
function.prototype: function RegKeyExists(const RootKey: HKEY; const SubKeyName: String): Boolean;

description.p: Returns True if the specified registry key exists.

function.name: RegValueExists
function.prototype: function RegValueExists(const RootKey: HKEY; const SubKeyName, ValueName: String): Boolean;

description.p: Returns True if the specified registry key and value exist.

function.name: RegGetSubkeyNames
function.prototype: function RegGetSubkeyNames(const RootKey: HKEY; const SubKeyName: String; var Names: TArrayOfString): Boolean;

description.p: Opens the specified registry key and reads the names of its subkeys into the specified string array Names. Returns True if successful, False otherwise.

function.name: RegGetValueNames
function.prototype: function RegGetValueNames(const RootKey: HKEY; const SubKeyName: String; var Names: TArrayOfString): Boolean;

description.p: Opens the specified registry key and reads the names of its values into the specified string array Names. Returns True if successful, False otherwise.

function.name: RegQueryStringValue
function.prototype: function RegQueryStringValue(const RootKey: HKEY; const SubKeyName, ValueName: String; var ResultStr: String): Boolean;

description.p: Queries the specified REG_SZ- or REG_EXPAND_SZ-type value, and returns the data in ResultStr. Returns True if successful. When False is returned, ResultStr is unmodified.

function.name: RegQueryMultiStringValue
function.prototype: function RegQueryMultiStringValue(const RootKey: HKEY; const SubKeyName, ValueName: String; var ResultStr: String): Boolean;

description.p: Queries the specified REG_MULTI_SZ-type registry value, and returns the data in ResultStr. Returns True if successful. When False is returned, ResultStr is unmodified.

remarks.p: In a REG_MULTI_SZ-type value, each string is separated by a null character (#0).

function.name: RegQueryDWordValue
function.prototype: function RegQueryDWordValue(const RootKey: HKEY; const SubKeyName, ValueName: String; var ResultDWord: Cardinal): Boolean;

description.p: Queries the specified REG_DWORD-type registry value, and returns the data in ResultDWord. Returns True if successful. When False is returned, ResultDWord is unmodified.

function.name: RegQueryBinaryValue
function.prototype: function RegQueryBinaryValue(const RootKey: HKEY; const SubKeyName, ValueName: String; var ResultStr: AnsiString): Boolean;

description.p: Queries the specified registry value, and returns the data in ResultStr. Returns True if successful. When False is returned, ResultStr is unmodified.

function.name: RegWriteStringValue
function.prototype: function RegWriteStringValue(const RootKey: HKEY; const SubKeyName, ValueName, Data: String): Boolean;

description.p: Writes the specified REG_SZ-type registry value. Returns True if successful, False otherwise.

remarks.p: If the value already exists and is of type REG_EXPAND_SZ, the new value will also be of type REG_EXPAND_SZ. Otherwise, a REG_SZ-type value will be created.

function.name: RegWriteExpandStringValue
function.prototype: function RegWriteExpandStringValue(const RootKey: HKEY; const SubKeyName, ValueName, Data: String): Boolean;

description.p: Writes the specified REG_EXPAND_SZ-type registry value. Returns True if successful, False otherwise.

function.name: RegWriteMultiStringValue
function.prototype: function RegWriteMultiStringValue(const RootKey: HKEY; const SubKeyName, ValueName, Data: String): Boolean;

description.p: Writes the specified REG_MULTI_SZ-type registry value. Returns True if successful, False otherwise.

remarks.p: In a REG_MULTI_SZ-type value, each string is separated by a null character (#0).

function.name: RegWriteDWordValue
function.prototype: function RegWriteDWordValue(const RootKey: HKEY; const SubKeyName, ValueName: String; const Data: Cardinal): Boolean;

description.p: Writes the specified REG_DWORD-type registry value. Returns True if successful, False otherwise.

function.name: RegWriteBinaryValue
function.prototype: function RegWriteBinaryValue(const RootKey: HKEY; const SubKeyName, ValueName: String; const Data: AnsiString): Boolean;

description.p: Writes the specified REG_BINARY-type registry value. Returns True if successful, False otherwise.

function.name: RegDeleteKeyIncludingSubkeys
function.prototype: function RegDeleteKeyIncludingSubkeys(const RootKey: HKEY; const SubkeyName: String): Boolean;

description.p: Deletes the specified key and all subkeys. Returns True if successful, False otherwise.

function.name: RegDeleteKeyIfEmpty
function.prototype: function RegDeleteKeyIfEmpty(const RootKey: HKEY; const SubkeyName: String): Boolean;

description.p: Deletes the specified subkey if it has no subkeys or values. Returns True if successful, False otherwise.

function.name: RegDeleteValue
function.prototype: function RegDeleteValue(const RootKey: HKEY; const SubKeyName, ValueName: String): Boolean;

description.p: Deletes the specified value. Returns True if successful, False otherwise.

function.name: IniKeyExists
function.prototype: function IniKeyExists(const Section, Key, Filename: String): Boolean;

description.p: Returns True if the specified INI key exists.

function.name: IsIniSectionEmpty
function.prototype: function IsIniSectionEmpty(const Section, Filename: String): Boolean;

description.p: Returns True if the specified INI section is empty.

function.name: GetIniBool
function.prototype: function GetIniBool(const Section, Key: String; const Default: Boolean; const Filename: String): Boolean;

description.p: Reads a Boolean from an INI file.

function.name: GetIniInt
function.prototype: function GetIniInt(const Section, Key: String; const Default, Min, Max: LongInt; const Filename: String): LongInt;

description.p: Reads a LongInt from an INI file. If the LongInt read is not between Min/Max then it returns Default. If Min=Max then Min/Max are ignored.

function.name: GetIniString
function.prototype: function GetIniString(const Section, Key, Default, Filename: String): String;

description.p: Reads a String from an INI file.

function.name: SetIniBool
function.prototype: function SetIniBool(const Section, Key: String; const Value: Boolean; const Filename: String): Boolean;

description.p: Writes a Boolean to an INI file.

function.name: SetIniInt
function.prototype: function SetIniInt(const Section, Key: String; const Value: LongInt; const Filename: String): Boolean;

description.p: Writes a LongInt to an INI file.

function.name: SetIniString
function.prototype: function SetIniString(const Section, Key, Value, Filename: String): Boolean;

description.p: Writes a string to an INI file.
Deletes the specified section from an INI file.

function.name: DeleteIniEntry
function.prototype: procedure DeleteIniEntry(const Section, Key, Filename: String);

description.p: Deletes the specified key from an INI file.

function.name: CreateInputQueryPage
function.prototype: function CreateInputQueryPage(const AfterID: Integer; const ACaption, ADescription, ASubCaption: String): TInputQueryWizardPage;

description.p: Creates a wizard page containing edit boxes.

remarks.p: To create edit boxes on the page, call the Add method. Use the Values property to get/set the text of the edit boxes.

example.p: See AllPagesExample.iss for another example.

function.name: CreateInputOptionPage
function.prototype: function CreateInputOptionPage(const AfterID: Integer; const ACaption, ADescription, ASubCaption: String; Exclusive, ListBox: Boolean): TInputOptionWizardPage;

description.p: Creates a wizard page containing check boxes or radio buttons.

If Exclusive is True, radio buttons are displayed instead of check boxes, and only one item in the list may be selected at a time. If ListBox is True, the check boxes or radio buttons are placed inside a scrollable list box.

remarks.p: To create check boxes / radio buttons on the page, call the Add method. Use the Values property to get/set the checked state of items. On pages created with Exclusive set to True, you can get/set the index of the one selected item via the SelectedValueIndex property.

example.p: See AllPagesExample.iss for another example.

function.name: CreateInputDirPage
function.prototype: function CreateInputDirPage(const AfterID: Integer; const ACaption, ADescription, ASubCaption: String; AAppendDir: Boolean; ANewFolderName: String): TInputDirWizardPage;

description.p: Creates a wizard page that contains edit boxes and Browse buttons for selecting directories. If AAppendDir is True, the value of ANewFolderName will be appended onto any folder name the user clicks. If AAppendDir is False and ANewFolderName is not empty, a Make New Folder button will be shown that creates a new folder with the specified default name.

remarks.p: To create directory selection boxes on the page, call the Add method. Use the Values property to get/set the items' values.

example.p: See AllPagesExample.iss for another example.

function.name: CreateInputFilePage
function.prototype: function CreateInputFilePage(const AfterID: Integer; const ACaption, ADescription, ASubCaption: String): TInputFileWizardPage;

description.p: Creates a wizard page that contains edit boxes and Browse buttons for selecting files.

remarks.p: To create file selection boxes on the page, call the Add method. Use the Values property to get/set the items' values.
An example Filter: 'Text files (*.txt)|*.txt|All files (*.*)|*.*'

example.p: See AllPagesExample.iss for another example.

function.name: CreateOutputMsgPage
function.prototype: function CreateOutputMsgPage(const AfterID: Integer; const ACaption, ADescription, AMsg: String): TOutputMsgWizardPage;

description.p: Creates a wizard page containing only static text. The AMsg parameter specifies the text to display.

example.p: See AllPagesExample.iss for another example.

function.name: CreateOutputMsgMemoPage
function.prototype: function CreateOutputMsgMemoPage(const AfterID: Integer; const ACaption, ADescription, ASubCaption: String; const AMsg: AnsiString): TOutputMsgMemoWizardPage;

description.p: Creates a wizard page containing static text as well as a read-only, multi-line edit control, capable of displaying RTF text. The ASubCaption parameter specifies the static text to display. AMsg specifies the text to assign to the edit control.

example.p: See AllPagesExample.iss for another example.

function.name: CreateOutputProgressPage
function.prototype: function CreateOutputProgressPage(const ACaption, ADescription: String): TOutputProgressWizardPage;

description.p: Creates a wizard page containing static text as well as a progress bar (which is hidden by default).

Unlike the other types of wizard pages, progress pages are not displayed as part of the normal page sequence (note that there is no AfterID parameter). A progress page can only be displayed programmatically by calling its Show method.

remarks.p: Call the Show method to activate and show the page. When you're finished with it, call the Hide method to revert to the previous page.

Always put the Hide call inside the finally part of a try..finally language construct, as demonstrated in CodeDlg.iss. Not calling Hide will result in the wizard being permanently stuck on the progress page.

To set the text on the page, call the SetText method. SetText takes two string parameters: use the first to tell the user what you're doing, and the second to display a file or directory name. Either parameter may be blank.

To show or update the progress bar, call the SetProgress method. SetProgress takes two integer parameters: the first specifies the position of the progress bar (zero-based), and the second specifies the highest possible position. If the second parameter is 0, the progress bar will be hidden.

example.p: See CodeDlg.iss and AllPagesExample.iss example scripts in the Examples subdirectory in your Inno Setup directory for examples.

function.name: CreateOutputMarqueeProgressPage
function.prototype: function CreateOutputMarqueeProgressPage(const ACaption, ADescription: String): TOutputMarqueeProgressWizardPage;

description.p: Creates a wizard page containing static text as well as a marquee progress bar.
See CreateOutputProgressPage for information on how to work with progress pages.

remarks.p: To animate the progress bar, call the Animate method. Do not call the SetProgress method, it will raise an internal error.

example.p: See AllPagesExample.iss for an example.

function.name: CreateDownloadPage
function.prototype: function CreateDownloadPage(const ACaption, ADescription: String; const OnDownloadProgress: TOnDownloadProgress): TDownloadWizardPage;

description.p: Creates a wizard page to download files and show progress.
Set OnDownloadProgress to a function to be informed of progress, or nil otherwise.

Unlike the other types of wizard pages, progress pages are not displayed as part of the normal page sequence (note that there is no AfterID parameter). A progress page can only be displayed programmatically by calling its Show method.

remarks.p: Call the Show method to activate and show the page. When you're finished with it, call the Hide method to revert to the previous page.

Always put the Hide call inside the finally part of a try..finally language construct, as demonstrated in CodeDownloadFiles.iss. Not calling Hide will result in the wizard being permanently stuck on the progress page.
To add a new file to download, call the Add method. Always call the Clear method before adding the first file.
To start the download, call the Download method. An exception will be raised if there was an error. Otherwise, Download returns the number of bytes downloaded.
Set the ShowBaseNameInsteadOfUrl property to True to show the base name of the file which is being downloaded to the user instead of the URL.
See DownloadTemporaryFile for other considerations and the definition of TOnDownloadProgress.
See CodeDownloadFiles.iss for an example.
See DownloadFiles.iss for an example of file download using just a [Files] entry instead.

function.name: CreateExtractionPage
function.prototype: function CreateExtractionPage(const ACaption, ADescription: String; const OnExtractionProgress: TOnExtractionProgress): TExtractionWizardPage;

description.p: Creates a wizard page to extract archives and show progress.
The supported archive formats, beyond .7z, and the support for password-protected and multi-volume archives, depend on the ArchiveExtraction [Setup] section directive.
To allow the extraction of archives with custom extensions, such as self-extracting archives, call MapArchiveExtensions.
Set OnExtractionProgress to a function to be informed of progress, or nil otherwise.

Unlike the other types of wizard pages, progress pages are not displayed as part of the normal page sequence (note that there is no AfterID parameter). A progress page can only be displayed programmatically by calling its Show method.

remarks.p: Call the Show method to activate and show the page. When you're finished with it, call the Hide method to revert to the previous page.

Always put the Hide call inside the finally part of a try..finally language construct, as demonstrated in CodeDownloadFiles.iss. Not calling Hide will result in the wizard being permanently stuck on the progress page.
To add a new archive to extract, call the Add method or the AddEx method if the archive is password-protected. Always call the Clear method before adding the first file.
To start the extraction, call the Extract method. An exception will be raised if there was an error.
Set the ShowArchiveInsteadOfFile property to True to show the name of the archive which is being extracted to the user instead of the names of the files inside the archive.
See ExtractArchive for the definition of TOnExtractionProgress.

example.p: See CodeDownloadFiles.iss for an example of CreateDownloadPage which works very similar to CreateExtractionPage.
See DownloadFiles.iss for an example of archive extraction using just a [Files] entry instead.

function.name: CreateCustomPage
function.prototype: function CreateCustomPage(const AfterID: Integer; const ACaption, ADescription: String): TWizardPage;

description.p: Creates a custom wizard page. The page is empty by default; you have to create your own controls afterward and place them on the page (by setting their Parent properties to the Surface property of the TWizardPage instance returned by this function).

example.p: See CodeClasses.iss and AllPagesExample.iss example scripts in the Examples subdirectory in your Inno Setup directory for examples.

function.name: CreateCustomForm
function.prototype: function CreateCustomForm(const ClientWidth, ClientHeight: Integer; const KeepSizeX, KeepSizeY: Boolean): TSetupForm;

description.p: Creates a form. The form is empty by default; you have to create your own controls afterward and place them on the form (by setting their Parent properties to the TSetupForm instance returned by this function).

remarks.p: You should call this function instead of creating TForm or TSetupForm instances directly. This function automatically initializes the font and other properties of the created form to be like Setup's other dialogs.
The [LangOptions] section's DialogFontName and DialogFontSize directives determine the font used by the form and, by default, any child controls created on the form.
You should use ScaleX and ScaleY to scale the desired client width and height to appropriate values. A form's client width and height are the form's full width and height minus its borders.
KeepSizeX and KeepSizeY specify whether the form is allowed to grow horizontally and vertically as the client area is resized, for [Setup] section directive WizardSizePercent.

The signature of the CreateCustomForm function has changed as of version 6.6.0. The width and height must now be specified upfront (they are now read-only properties once the form is constructed). Previously, your code might have looked like this:
Starting with version 6.6.0, it should be updated as follows:

example.pre: Form := CreateCustomForm(ScaleX(256), ScaleY(128), False, False); Form.Caption := 'TSetupForm';

remarks.p: The same applies to the KeepSizeX and KeepSizeY properties, which were writable properties before version 6.6.0, but are now specified as parameters when calling CreateCustomForm and are read-only properties afterwards.

example.p: See CodeClasses.iss for an example.

description.p: Given a page ID, returns a TWizardPage instance. Call this if, for example, you need to get at the surface of a page and only know its ID.
An exception will be raised if an invalid page ID is specified.
Given a page ID, returns a position index. Call this if, for example, you want to check whether a page is positioned before a certain other page.
Takes an X coordinate or width and returns it scaled to fit the size of the current dialog font. If you use the default font settings with standard DPI (100%), then X is returned unchanged.
Takes a Y coordinate or height and returns it scaled to fit the size of the current dialog font. If you use the default font settings with standard DPI (100%), then Y is returned unchanged.

function.name: InitializeBitmapButtonFromIcon
function.prototype: function InitializeBitmapButtonFromIcon(const BitmapButton: TBitmapButton; const IconFilename: String; const BkColor: TColor; const AscendingTrySizes: TArrayOfInteger): Boolean;

description.p: Initializes the given bitmap button with an icon from the given icon file using the given background color for transparent parts.
See InitializeBitmapImageFromIcon for more information.

function.name: InitializeBitmapImageFromIcon
function.prototype: function InitializeBitmapImageFromIcon(const BitmapImage: TBitmapImage; const IconFilename: String; const BkColor: TColor; const AscendingTrySizes: TArrayOfInteger): Boolean;

description.p: Initializes the given bitmap image with an icon from the given icon file using the given background color for transparent parts.

The bitmap image should be scaled already and the function will load the largest fitting icon which has a size from the given array of sizes. After loading the function will set the size of the bitmap image to the loaded size.
The array must be sorted already from smallest to highest size.
If no match is found in the array (for example, because it is empty), the minimum of the bitmap image's width and height will be used as the size.
Returns True if the icon could be loaded, False otherwise.

function.name: InitializeBitmapButtonFromStockIcon
function.prototype: function InitializeBitmapButtonFromStockIcon(const BitmapButton: TBitmapButton; const Siid: Integer; const BkColor: TColor; const AscendingTrySizes: TArrayOfInteger): Boolean;

description.p: Initializes the given bitmap button with a system-defined stock icon from the given stock icon id (Siid) using the given background color for transparent parts.
See InitializeBitmapImageFromStockIcon for more information.

function.name: InitializeBitmapImageFromStockIcon
function.prototype: function InitializeBitmapImageFromStockIcon(const BitmapImage: TBitmapImage; const Siid: Integer; const BkColor: TColor; const AscendingTrySizes: TArrayOfInteger): Boolean;

description.p: Initializes the given bitmap image with a system-defined stock icon from the given stock icon id (Siid) using the given background color for transparent parts.

The bitmap image should be scaled already and the function will load the largest fitting icon which has a size from the given array of sizes. After loading the function will set the size of the bitmap image to the loaded size.
The array must be sorted already from smallest to highest size.
If no match is found in the array (for example, because it is empty), the minimum of the bitmap image's width and height will be used as the size.
Unlike InitializeBitmapImageFromIcon, this function supports arbitrary sizes.
Returns True if the icon could be loaded, False otherwise.

remarks.p: See Microsoft Learn for an overview of all available icons.
Supported values for Siid are:

p.tt: SIID_DOCNOASSOC, SIID_DOCASSOC, SIID_APPLICATION, SIID_FOLDER, SIID_FOLDEROPEN, SIID_DRIVE525, SIID_DRIVE35, SIID_DRIVEREMOVE, SIID_DRIVEFIXED, SIID_DRIVENET, SIID_DRIVENETDISABLED, SIID_DRIVECD, SIID_DRIVERAM, SIID_WORLD, SIID_SERVER, SIID_PRINTER, SIID_MYNETWORK, SIID_FIND, SIID_HELP, SIID_SHARE, SIID_LINK, SIID_SLOWFILE, SIID_RECYCLER, SIID_RECYCLERFULL, SIID_MEDIACDAUDIO, SIID_LOCK, SIID_AUTOLIST, SIID_PRINTERNET, SIID_SERVERSHARE, SIID_PRINTERFAX, SIID_PRINTERFAXNET, SIID_PRINTERFILE, SIID_STACK, SIID_MEDIASVCD, SIID_STUFFEDFOLDER, SIID_DRIVEUNKNOWN, SIID_DRIVEDVD, SIID_MEDIADVD, SIID_MEDIADVDRAM, SIID_MEDIADVDRW, SIID_MEDIADVDR, SIID_MEDIADVDROM, SIID_MEDIACDAUDIOPLUS, SIID_MEDIACDRW, SIID_MEDIACDR, SIID_MEDIACDBURN, SIID_MEDIABLANKCD, SIID_MEDIACDROM, SIID_AUDIOFILES, SIID_IMAGEFILES, SIID_VIDEOFILES, SIID_MIXEDFILES, SIID_FOLDERBACK, SIID_FOLDERFRONT, SIID_SHIELD, SIID_WARNING, SIID_INFO, SIID_ERROR, SIID_KEY, SIID_SOFTWARE, SIID_RENAME, SIID_DELETE, SIID_MEDIAAUDIODVD, SIID_MEDIAMOVIEDVD, SIID_MEDIAENHANCEDCD, SIID_MEDIAENHANCEDDVD, SIID_MEDIAHDDVD, SIID_MEDIABLURAY, SIID_MEDIAVCD, SIID_MEDIADVDPLUSR, SIID_MEDIADVDPLUSRW, SIID_DESKTOPPC, SIID_MOBILEPC, SIID_USERS, SIID_MEDIASMARTMEDIA, SIID_MEDIACOMPACTFLASH, SIID_DEVICECELLPHONE, SIID_DEVICECAMERA, SIID_DEVICEVIDEOCAMERA, SIID_DEVICEAUDIOPLAYER, SIID_NETWORKCONNECT, SIID_INTERNET, SIID_ZIPFILE, SIID_SETTINGS, SIID_DRIVEHDDVD, SIID_DRIVEBD, SIID_MEDIAHDDVDROM, SIID_MEDIAHDDVDR, SIID_MEDIAHDDVDRAM, SIID_MEDIABDROM, SIID_MEDIABDR, SIID_MEDIABDRE, SIID_CLUSTEREDDRIVE

function.name: MsgBox
function.prototype: function MsgBox(const Text: String; const Typ: TMsgBoxType; const Buttons: Integer): Integer;

description.p: Displays a message box. Text specifies the message to display. Typ specifies which icon to display in the message box. Buttons specifies which buttons to include in the message box. Returns an ID* constant indicating the button the user clicked, or 0 if the function fails (which shouldn't happen unless an invalid parameter is specified or system resources are exhausted).

remarks.p: TMsgBoxType is defined as:
Supported flags for Buttons are:

p.tt: MB_OK, MB_OKCANCEL, MB_ABORTRETRYIGNORE, MB_YESNOCANCEL, MB_YESNO, MB_RETRYCANCEL, MB_DEFBUTTON1, MB_DEFBUTTON2, MB_DEFBUTTON3, MB_SETFOREGROUND

remarks.p: Possible return values are:

p.tt: IDOK, IDCANCEL, IDABORT, IDRETRY, IDIGNORE, IDYES, IDNO

function.name: SuppressibleMsgBox
function.prototype: function SuppressibleMsgBox(const Text: String; const Typ: TMsgBoxType; const Buttons, Default: Integer): Integer;

description.p: Displays a suppressible message box. If message boxes are being suppressed (see Setup Command-Line Parameters), Default is returned. Otherwise, SuppressibleMsgBox acts the same as the regular MsgBox.

function.name: TaskDialogMsgBox
function.prototype: function TaskDialogMsgBox(const Instruction, Text: String; const Typ: TMsgBoxType; const Buttons: Cardinal; const ButtonLabels: TArrayOfString; const ShieldButton: Integer): Integer;

description.p:
Displays a message box using a task dialog:
Instruction specifies the instruction to display.
Text specifies the message to display.
Typ specifies which icon to display in the task dialog. If set to mbConfirmation, no icon will be displayed.
Buttons specifies which buttons to include in the task dialog.
ButtonLabels specifies which custom button labels to use. If set to an empty array, the system's default button labels will be used. If a label consists of two strings separated by a newline, then the first string specifies the button label and the second string specifies the button note. If Buttons is set to MB_OKCANCEL, MB_YESNOCANCEL, or MB_RETRYCANCEL, specifying a label for the Cancel button as the last element in the array is optional.
ShieldButton specifies which button to display a shield icon on. If set to 0, no shield icon will be displayed.
Returns an ID* constant indicating the button the user clicked, or 0 if the function fails (which shouldn't happen unless an invalid parameter is specified or system resources are exhausted).

remarks.p: TMsgBoxType is defined as:
Supported values for Buttons are:

p.tt: MB_OK, MB_OKCANCEL, MB_YESNOCANCEL, MB_YESNO, MB_RETRYCANCEL, MB_ABORTRETRYIGNORE

remarks.p: If MB_ABORTRETRYIGNORE is used, ButtonLabels may not be an empty array and the button labels must be specified in the following special order: Retry, Ignore, Abort.
Supported values for ShieldButton and possible return values are:

p.tt: IDOK, IDCANCEL, IDRETRY, IDYES, IDNO, IDABORT, IDIGNORE

function.name: SuppressibleTaskDialogMsgBox
function.prototype: function SuppressibleTaskDialogMsgBox(const Instruction, Text: String; const Typ: TMsgBoxType; const Buttons: Cardinal; const ButtonLabels: TArrayOfString; const ShieldButton: Integer; const Default: Integer): Integer;

description.p: Displays a suppressible message box using a task dialog. If message boxes are being suppressed (see Setup Command-Line Parameters), Default is returned. Otherwise, SuppressibleTaskDialogMsgBox acts the same as the regular TaskDialogMsgBox.

function.name: GetOpenFileName
function.prototype: function GetOpenFileName(const Prompt: String; var FileName: String; const InitialDirectory, Filter, DefaultExtension: String): Boolean;

description.p: Displays a dialog box that enables the user to select an existing file. Returns True if the user selected a file, False otherwise. The name of the selected file is returned in the FileName string.

remarks.p: An example Filter: 'Text files (*.txt)|*.txt|All files (*.*)|*.*'

function.name: GetOpenFileNameMulti
function.prototype: function GetOpenFileNameMulti(const Prompt: String; const FileNameList: TStrings; const InitialDirectory, Filter, DefaultExtension: String): Boolean;

description.p: Displays a dialog box that enables the user to select one or more existing file(s). Returns True if the user selected a file, False otherwise. The name of the selected file(s) is returned in the FileNameList list.

remarks.p: An example Filter: 'Text files (*.txt)|*.txt|All files (*.*)|*.*'

function.name: GetSaveFileName
function.prototype: function GetSaveFileName(const Prompt: String; var FileName: String; const InitialDirectory, Filter, DefaultExtension: String): Boolean;

description.p: Displays a dialog box that enables the user to select a new file. Returns True if the user selected a file, False otherwise. The name of the selected file is returned in the FileName string.

remarks.p: An example Filter: 'Text files (*.txt)|*.txt|All files (*.*)|*.*'

function.name: BrowseForFolder
function.prototype: function BrowseForFolder(const Prompt: String; var Directory: String; const Reserved: Boolean): Boolean;

description.p: Displays a dialog box that enables the user to select a directory. The current value of Directory is used as the initially selected directory. Returns True if the user selected a directory and clicked OK, False otherwise. The selected directory is returned in the Directory string.
Parameter Reserved is ignored, but must be specified anyway.
Displays the "Exit Setup?" message box, and returns True if the user selects Yes. Does not terminate Setup or Uninstall.

function.name: SelectDisk
function.prototype: function SelectDisk(const DiskNumber: Integer; const AFilename: String; var Path: String): Boolean;

description.p: Displays the "Setup Needs the Next Disk" message box, and returns True if the user enters a path containing the file specified by AFilename and selects OK. The selected path is returned in the Path string. The current value of Path is used as the initially selected path and DiskNumber is used only as information to the user.
See the Using COM Automation objects topic.
See the Using COM Automation objects topic.

function.name: IDispatchInvoke
function.prototype: function IDispatchInvoke(Self: IDispatch; PropertySet: Boolean; const Name: String; Par: array of Variant): Variant;

description.p: Use IDispatchInvoke to access a COM Automation property or method whose name is a reserved word.
See the Using COM Automation objects topic.
StringToGUID converts the string representation of a GUID into a 'real' GUID. An exception will be raised upon failure.
Use OleCheck to wrap any IUnknown based COM methods you call, so that if that method fails, an exception will be raised.
OleCheck is called automatically when the method declaration specifies the safecall calling convention.
See the Using COM Automation objects topic.
Logs the specified string in Setup's or Uninstall's log file and/or in the Compiler IDE's "Debug Output" view.

remarks.p: Calls to this function are ignored if logging is not enabled (via the /LOG command-line parameter or the SetupLogging [Setup] section directive or the UninstallLogging [Setup] section directive or debugging from the Compiler IDE).

function.name: LogFmt
function.prototype: procedure LogFmt(const S: String; const Args: array of const);

description.p: Formats the string S using the series of arguments in the open array Args, and then logs it.

function.name: ExecAndCaptureOutput
function.prototype: function ExecAndCaptureOutput(const Filename, Params, WorkingDir: String; const ShowCmd: Integer; const Wait: TExecWait; var ResultCode: Integer; var Output: TExecOutput): Boolean;

description.p: Identical to Exec except:
Program output from the stdout and stderr streams is captured. This is different from ExecAndLogOutput which merges the streams.
Console programs are always hidden and the ShowCmd parameter only affects GUI programs, so always using SW_SHOWNORMAL instead of SW_HIDE is recommended.
An exception will be raised if there was an error setting up output redirection (which should be very rare).

remarks.p: Parameter Wait must always be set to ewWaitUntilTerminated when calling this function.
TExecOutput is defined as:

Error will be True if there was an error reading the output (which should be very rare) or if the output is too big. The error is logged in Setup's or Uninstall's log file and/or in the Compiler IDE's "Debug Output" view. There is no further output after an error.
Output is limited to a total size of 10 million bytes or a maximum of 1 million lines.

function.name: ExecAndLogOutput
function.prototype: function ExecAndLogOutput(const Filename, Params, WorkingDir: String; const ShowCmd: Integer; const Wait: TExecWait; var ResultCode: Integer; const OnLog: TOnLog): Boolean;

description.p: Identical to Exec except:
Console programs are always hidden and the ShowCmd parameter only affects GUI programs, so always using SW_SHOWNORMAL instead of SW_HIDE is recommended.
If OnLog is set to nil then the output of the executed executable or batch file is logged in Setup's or Uninstall's log file and/or in the Compiler IDE's "Debug Output" view.
If OnLog is not set to nil then the output is sent to the specified function, line by line.
An exception will be raised if there was an error setting up output redirection (which should be very rare).

remarks.p: Parameter Wait must always be set to ewWaitUntilTerminated when calling this function.
TOnLog is defined as:

p.tt: TOnLog = procedure(const S: String; const Error, FirstLine: Boolean);

remarks.p: Parameter S is the output line when Error is False, otherwise an error message. FirstLine is True if this is the first line of output from the program, otherwise False.
Error will be True if reading the output failed (which should be very rare), or if the output is too big. There is no further output after an error.
Output is limited to a total size of 10 million bytes or a maximum of 1 million lines.

function.name: ExecAndCaptureOutputWithNativeSysDir
function.prototype: function ExecAndCaptureOutputWithNativeSysDir(const Filename, Params, WorkingDir: String; const ShowCmd: Integer; const Wait: TExecWait; var ResultCode: Integer; var Output: TExecOutput): Boolean;

description.p: Use this function instead of ExecAndCaptureOutput to launch an executable located in the 64-bit System directory from a 32-bit installer.

This function avoids the problem that ExecAndCaptureOutput in a 32-bit installer can only launch such an executable using the Sysnative alias, while that alias is not available to 64-bit applications. To achieve this, the function temporarily disables WOW64 file system redirection.

remarks.p: On 32-bit Windows or in a 64-bit installer, this function is just an alias for ExecAndCaptureOutput.

function.name: ExecAndLogOutputWithNativeSysDir
function.prototype: function ExecAndLogOutputWithNativeSysDir(const Filename, Params, WorkingDir: String; const ShowCmd: Integer; const Wait: TExecWait; var ResultCode: Integer; const OnLog: TOnLog): Boolean;

description.p: Use this function instead of ExecAndLogOutput to launch an executable located in the 64-bit System directory from a 32-bit installer.

This function avoids the problem that ExecAndLogOutput in a 32-bit installer can only launch such an executable using the Sysnative alias, while that alias is not available to 64-bit applications. To achieve this, the function temporarily disables WOW64 file system redirection.

remarks.p: On 32-bit Windows or in a 64-bit installer, this function is just an alias for ExecAndLogOutput.

description.p: Suspends the execution of Setup or Uninstall for a specified interval.
Returns a random number within the range 0 <= X < Range.
Beeps.

example.pre: Beep; //Beeps

description.p: Returns the absolute value of E.
Returns the value of E rounded to the integer number.

remarks.p: If E is exactly halfway between two integer numbers, the result is always the even number. This method of rounding is often called "Banker's rounding."

description.p: Returns the truncated value of E; that is, E rounded toward zero, as an integer number.
Returns the integer part of E; that is, E rounded toward zero.

function.name: MulDiv
function.prototype: function MulDiv(const Number, Numerator, Denominator: Integer): Integer;

description.p: Multiplies two 32-bit values (Number and Numerator) and then divides the 64-bit result by a third 32-bit value (Denominator). The final result is rounded to the nearest integer.

remarks.p: If the result is a positive half integer (ends in .5), it is rounded up. If the result is a negative half integer, it is rounded down.

description.p: Sets the FPU control word.

example.pre: Set8087CW($133f); // Disables all FPU exceptions

description.p: Returns the current value of the FPU control word.
Returns the lowest value or lower bound of X. Cannot be used with type parameters.
Returns the highest value or upper bound of X. Cannot be used with type parameters.
Returns the number of bytes occupied by X. Cannot be used with type parameters.
Returns True if X is not nil. For strings and PChar values, returns True if X isn't empty. Returns False otherwise.
Increments X by 1.
Decrements X by 1.
Returns the successor of X.
Returns the predecessor of X.

function.name: Include
function.prototype: procedure Include(var S: Set; I: Ordinal);

description.p: Adds an element to a set.

function.name: Exclude
function.prototype: procedure Exclude(var S: Set; I: Ordinal);

description.p: Removes an element from a set.
Makes sure that Setup or Uninstall is visible and the foreground window.

function.name: LoadDLL
function.prototype: function LoadDLL(const DLLName: String; var ErrorCode: Integer): HMODULE;

description.p: Loads the specified DLL. Returns the DLL handle if the DLL was loaded successfully, zero otherwise. If zero is returned then ErrorCode specifies the error that occurred. Use SysErrorMessage(ErrorCode) to get a description of the error.

remarks.p: This function is deprecated. See the Using DLLs topic.
Refer to the system error codes on MSDN.

function.name: CallDLLProc
function.prototype: function CallDLLProc(const DLLHandle: HMODULE; const ProcName: String; const Param1, Param2: NativeInt; var Result: NativeInt): Boolean;

description.p:
Calls the specified function in a DLL specified using the DLL handle returned by LoadDLL. Returns True if the procedure was called successfully, False otherwise.
The function must use the standard calling convention, accept two NativeInt parameters and return a NativeInt result.

remarks.p: This function is deprecated. See the Using DLLs topic.

description.p: Unloads a DLL specified using the DLL handle returned by LoadDLL.

remarks.p: This function is deprecated. See the Using DLLs topic.

description.p: Casts a string to an integer so that a string can be passed to a DLL using CallDLLProc.

remarks.p: This function is deprecated. See the Using DLLs topic.

description.p: Casts an integer to a string so that a string can be received from a DLL using CallDLLProc.

remarks.p: This function is deprecated. See the Using DLLs topic.

enum.values: SW_SHOW, SW_SHOWNORMAL, SW_SHOWMAXIMIZED, SW_SHOWMINIMIZED, SW_SHOWMINNOACTIVE, SW_HIDE
MB_OK, MB_OKCANCEL, MB_ABORTRETRYIGNORE, MB_YESNOCANCEL, MB_YESNO, MB_RETRYCANCEL, MB_DEFBUTTON1, MB_DEFBUTTON2, MB_DEFBUTTON3, MB_SETFOREGROUND
